element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation piece, teaches rotation and line clears
Teaches edge placement and rotation utility
Escalates time pressure as player advances

typical_relief_triggers: Piece placement without stack reaching top
typical_tension_triggers: Stack height increasing toward danger zone
Fall speed increase at level advancement
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exponential tension escalation with level progression; breathing room abundant early, compressed dramatically in late game

difficulty_curve.early_game: Permissive; pieces fall slowly, ample time for placement decisions, learning tetrominoes and basic strategies
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Steady acceleration; fall speed increases, player must optimize placement, line-clear combos become important
difficulty_curve.late_game: Extreme pressure; fall speed near-instantaneous, single mistakes cascade into stack collapse, requires expert-level reflexes and prediction
notable_spikes: Level 10+ where fall speed transitions from manageable to punishing
Each subsequent level multiplies difficulty exponentially

notable_level_patterns.description: No discrete level reset or relief; difficulty increases monotonically via fall-speed acceleration and gravity intensification
Tetromino delivery unpredictable but seeded; player cannot rely on memorization, must adapt to incoming pieces
Playfield height (20 rows) acts as implicit timer; stack approaching top forces escalating decision speed

note: Available source (TCRF) does not contain actionable game design data for Tetris Genesis. Design patterns inferred from core Tetris mechanics (universal across ports). No specific Genesis version design documentation provided.
